text: What is the purpose of the 'PURGE' option in a COPY command?

options.a: It deletes the data from the Snowflake table after loading.
options.b: It removes the source files from the stage after a successful load.
options.c: It clears the query cache.
options.d: It drops the table after the load is finished.

text: True or False: Fail-safe is intended for use in accidental data deletion by users.
Which command allows a user to see the internal structure of a staged file without loading it?

options.c: SELECT from @stage

text: What is a 'Resource Monitor' primarily used for?

options.c: Managing and limiting credit consumption by warehouses.

text: True or False: Snowflake can run on a private cloud (on-premises).
Which metadata is stored for each micro-partition? (Choose three.)

options.c: The actual row data.

text: What is the standard size of a micro-partition before compression?

options.c: 1 GB

text: True or False: Data shared via a Snowflake Share can be updated by the Consumer.
Which function is used to convert a JSON string into a VARIANT type?

options.c: CAST_AS_JSON()

text: True or False: Snowflake provides a way to restore a table that was accidentally dropped using the UNDROP command.
Which of the following describes a 'Transient' table?

options.a: It exists only for the duration of a single session.
options.b: It has no Fail-safe period but persists across sessions.
options.c: It is a read-only copy of a Permanent table.
options.d: It is stored in the Cloud Services layer instead of the Storage layer.

text: When unloading data, what does the 'MAX_FILE_SIZE' parameter control?

options.a: The maximum size of the total data set to be unloaded.
options.b: The maximum upper limit of an individual file size created on the stage.
options.c: The maximum number of rows per file.
options.d: The compression ratio of the files.

text: True or False: A Virtual Warehouse can be configured to automatically scale based on the number of queries in the queue.
Which command is used to see the list of files currently residing in an Internal Stage?

options.c: SELECT * FROM @stage_name

text: Which role is primarily responsible for creating and managing users and roles within the RBAC hierarchy?

options.c: PUBLIC

text: What is the primary benefit of 'Search Optimization Service' in Snowflake?

options.a: It speeds up large table scans.
options.b: It improves the performance of point lookup queries on large tables.
options.c: It reduces the cost of data storage.
options.d: It automates the creation of clustering keys.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of standard SQL for querying semi-structured data like XML and JSON.
Which of the following are characteristics of a 'Temporary' table? (Choose two.)

options.a: It persists after the session ends.
options.b: It is only visible to the user who created it.
options.c: It does not incur any storage costs.
options.d: It is dropped automatically at the end of the session.

text: What is the maximum Time Travel retention period for a Transient table?

options.c: 7 days

text: Which function allows you to look into the contents of a VARIANT column and produce a set of rows (one for each element)?

options.c: SPLIT()

text: True or False: Data in Snowflake is encrypted using a hierarchical key model managed by Snowflake.
How does Snowflake charge for the Cloud Services layer?

options.b: It is charged only if usage exceeds 10% of daily compute credits.
options.c: It is a flat monthly fee.
options.d: It is charged per user per hour.

text: What does 'Clustering Depth' indicate for a table?

options.a: The number of columns in the clustering key.
options.b: How well the table is partitioned relative to the clustering key.
options.c: The total size of the micro-partitions.
options.d: The time it takes to scan the table.

text: True or False: You can share a secure view that joins data from multiple databases within your account.
Which tool is the primary CLI (Command Line Interface) for connecting to Snowflake?

options.c: SnowTerm

text: What happens to the 'Load History' of a table if the table is truncated?

options.c: The load history is reset for that day only.
options.d: The table cannot be truncated if it has load history.

text: True or False: Multi-cluster warehouses are available in the Standard Edition of Snowflake.
Which view provides information about the credits consumed by 'Auto-Clustering'?

options.c: WAREHOUSE_METERING_HISTORY

text: What is the primary difference between a 'Standard' and an 'Enterprise' edition account regarding Time Travel?

options.b: Standard is limited to 1 day; Enterprise allows up to 90 days.
options.c: Standard allows up to 90 days; Enterprise allows up to 365 days.

text: Which statement is true regarding Snowflake's support for ACID properties?

options.a: Snowflake only supports Atomic and Consistent properties.
options.c: Snowflake is ACID compliant only for single-row inserts.
options.d: Snowflake does not support ACID properties.

text: True or False: To use the 'PUT' command, you must use a Snowflake client like SnowSQL or a driver.
Which column in the Query History page helps identify the Warehouse that ran the query?

options.c: Compute Resource

text: What is the impact of a 'Full Outer Join' on two very large tables that are not clustered similarly?

options.a: High probability of query failing due to timeout.
options.b: Increased probability of spilling data to remote storage.
options.c: The query will automatically trigger auto-clustering.
options.d: No impact, Snowflake handles this via Cloud Services.

text: True or False: When sharing data, the Data Provider pays for the storage and the Data Consumer pays for the compute used to query the shared data.
Which Snowflake object allows you to group multiple SQL statements together and execute them as a single unit?

options.c: UDF

text: In a Multi-cluster Warehouse, what does the 'Economy' scaling policy prioritize?

options.b: Conserving credits by keeping clusters busy.
options.c: Automatically resizing clusters.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the creation of 'External Tables' that query data sitting in an S3 bucket without importing it.
Which of the following describes 'Fail-safe' storage? (Choose two.)

options.a: It is configurable by the user.
options.b: It provides 7 days of additional protection after Time Travel.
options.c: It can be used to recover data from any point in the last 90 days.
options.d: It can only be accessed by Snowflake Support.

text: What is the primary reason for a 'Virtual Warehouse' to be in a 'Starting' state for a long period?

options.a: Wait time for cloud provider to provision resources.
options.b: The warehouse is too small for the query.
options.c: MFA is required to start the warehouse.
options.d: The database is currently being cloned.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows you to define a clustering key on a view.
Which of the following is true about a Snowflake 'Share' object? (Choose two.)

options.a: It contains the actual data being shared.
options.b: It contains references to the database objects being shared.
options.c: It can be modified by the Consumer account.
options.d: It identifies the accounts that can access the shared data.

text: How many days of Time Travel are provided for 'Temporary' tables?

options.c: 7 days

text: Which command is used to update the data in a Snowflake table by joining it with another table or a stage?

options.c: UPSERT

text: True or False: Data in Snowflake is automatically compressed using an proprietary algorithm designed for columnar storage.
What is the primary function of the 'Cloud Services' layer in Snowflake's architecture?

options.b: To execute the heavy compute of a query.
options.c: To manage metadata, security, and infrastructure coordination.
options.d: To provide the web user interface.

text: Which role is recommended for daily administrative tasks such as creating warehouses and databases?

options.c: SECURITYADMIN

text: True or False: Snowflake supports the 'Parquet' file format for both loading and unloading data.
Which feature allows you to 'time-travel' to a specific point in time to see the data as it existed then?

options.c: SELECT ... AT

text: What does it mean if a Virtual Warehouse is 'Provisioning'?

options.a: It is currently executing a query.
options.b: The cloud provider is allocating the necessary hardware resources.
options.c: It is waiting for more queries to enter the queue.
options.d: It is being resized to a smaller size.

text: True or False: An Internal Stage can be used to share files with other Snowflake accounts directly.
Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization'?

options.a: It indexes every column in a table automatically.
options.b: It uses a persistent data structure to speed up specific types of point lookup queries.
options.c: It is a free service provided in all Snowflake editions.
options.d: It requires the user to manually refresh the index.

text: What is the maximum number of clusters a 'Standard' edition warehouse can have?
True or False: Snowflake credits are consumed for the time a warehouse is in a 'Suspended' state.
Which function is used to convert a VARIANT value back into a specific data type like INT or VARCHAR?

options.c: DECODE()

text: What is 'Tri-Secret Secure' in Snowflake?

options.a: A security model involving a combination of a Snowflake-managed key, a customer-managed key, and the data itself.
options.c: A method for sharing data with three different accounts.
options.d: A backup strategy using three different cloud regions.

text: True or False: You can clone an individual table across different Snowflake accounts.
When using 'Snowpipe', how are you billed for the compute resources?

options.a: Based on the size of the warehouse you assigned to it.
options.b: Based on the actual resource consumption (per-second) used for the file ingestion.
options.c: A flat monthly fee per pipe.
options.d: Snowpipe is free; you only pay for storage.

text: Which of the following objects can have 'Tags' applied to them for cost tracking? (Choose three.)

options.c: User

text: True or False: A 'Secure View' prevents users from seeing the underlying SQL definition of the view.
What happens if a query exceeds the 'Statement Timeout' parameter set on a warehouse?

options.a: The warehouse is automatically resized to a larger size.
options.b: The query is aborted by the system.
options.c: The query is moved to a lower-priority queue.
options.d: The user is prompted to pay additional credits to continue.

text: Which Snowflake edition is required to access 'Database Replication' and 'Failover'?

options.c: Business Critical

text: True or False: If you clone a database, the new database is completely independent, and changes in the original will not reflect in the clone.
Which of the following is a key advantage of 'Columnar' storage?

options.b: It allows the system to read only the columns needed for a query, reducing I/O.
options.c: It eliminates the need for any encryption.
options.d: It requires more storage space than row-based storage.

text: True or False: You can use the 'GET' command in a web-based Worksheet to download files from an Internal Stage.
What is the role of a 'Service Account' in Snowflake?

options.a: It is an account used by Snowflake Support to fix bugs.
options.b: It is a standard Snowflake user account used by automated tools or applications to connect.
options.c: It is a special type of account that doesn't consume credits.
options.d: It is used to manage billing only.

text: Which command allows you to view the privileges granted to a specific role?

options.c: DESCRIBE ROLE [role_name]

text: True or False: Snowflake's 'Auto-Clustering' service incurs separate credit costs from the Virtual Warehouse compute.
Which 'Scaling Policy' should be used if you want to prioritize cluster availability and minimize query queuing in a multi-cluster warehouse?

options.c: Performance

text: What is a 'Materialized View' in Snowflake primarily used for?

options.a: To hide the complexity of a query from users.
options.b: To improve query performance for workloads that repeatedly use the same subquery results.
options.c: To store data in an external S3 bucket.
options.d: To share data with external users who don't have Snowflake.

text: True or False: Snowflake provides a way to verify the data loading history for the last 64 days using the LOAD_HISTORY view.
Which of the following describes the 'Multi-cluster' feature of a Virtual Warehouse?

options.a: It allows a warehouse to store data across multiple cloud regions.
options.b: It allows a warehouse to automatically add or remove execution clusters to handle varying concurrency demands.
options.c: It allows a warehouse to connect to multiple Snowflake accounts at once.
options.d: It allows a warehouse to process different data formats in separate clusters.

text: What is the result of using a 'Zero-copy clone' on a database that has Time Travel data?

options.a: The Time Travel data is copied to the new database.
options.b: The new database starts with its own Time Travel history from the moment of creation; it does not inherit the original's history.
options.c: Cloning is not possible if Time Travel is enabled.
options.d: The original database's Time Travel data is deleted to save space.

text: Which object in Snowflake is used to define the structure of a semi-structured data file during the loading process?

options.c: Stage Definition

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Directory Tables' to provide a SQL interface for querying metadata about files in a stage.
What is the primary purpose of the 'SYSTEM$PIPE_STATUS' function?

options.a: To see how many credits a pipe has consumed.
options.b: To determine the current execution state of a Snowpipe.
options.c: To see the list of files a pipe has loaded.
options.d: To manually start a suspended pipe.

text: True or False: Snowflake supports 'Pushdown Optimization' when using connectors like Spark to ensure processing happens inside Snowflake where possible.
Which function would you use to find the 'Clustering Depth' of a table to see if it needs a clustering key?

options.c: SHOW CLUSTERING_DEPTH

text: What is 'Dynamic Data Masking' in Snowflake?

options.a: A way to encrypt data files on a stage.
options.b: A security feature that hides sensitive data in query results based on the user's role.
options.c: A method for automatically compressing data.
options.d: A feature to hide the names of tables from unauthorized users.

text: True or False: You can set a 'Resource Monitor' at the Account level to control the total credit usage for the entire Snowflake account.
Which of the following statements about 'Micro-partitions' is true?

options.a: They are created and managed manually by the user.
options.b: They allow for efficient 'Pruning' during query execution.
options.c: They are stored in the Cloud Services layer.
options.d: They can be resized by the user to improve performance.

text: What happens if you try to query a 'Temporary' table from a different session than the one that created it?

options.a: The query succeeds if you have the correct role permissions.
options.b: The table will not be visible, and the query will fail.
options.c: The session will automatically join the original session.
options.d: The query will return an error saying the table is currently locked.

text: True or False: Snowflake charges for 'Cloud Services' usage if it exceeds 10% of the total daily compute credits consumed.
Which role is the only one that can manage 'Resource Monitors' by default?

options.c: ACCOUNTADMIN

text: Which command is used to move data from a Snowflake table into a file on a stage (unloading)?

options.c: EXPORT TO @stage

text: True or False: A 'Transient' database can contain 'Permanent' tables.
What is the benefit of 'External Functions' in Snowflake?

options.a: They allow you to write SQL functions that run on other Snowflake accounts.
options.b: They allow Snowflake to call code that runs outside of Snowflake, such as on AWS Lambda or Azure Functions.
options.c: They are used to share data with non-Snowflake users.
options.d: They provide faster query performance for large joins.

text: Which view in the Information Schema can be used to monitor Snowpipe file ingestion history?

options.c: COPY_HISTORY

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of 'Object Tagging' to categorize and track the cost of various Snowflake objects.
What is the 'Query Profile' tool used for?

options.a: To see the list of all users who ran a specific query.
options.b: To visualize and analyze the execution plan and performance of a query.
options.c: To set the default warehouse for a user.
options.d: To manage the security settings of a database.

text: Which of the following file types is NOT supported for natively loading into Snowflake?

options.c: MP3

text: True or False: When a Virtual Warehouse is resized, the change is applied immediately to all queries that are currently running.
What is a 'Database Role'?

options.a: A role that can only be used by the Database Administrator.
options.b: A role created within a specific database to manage privileges on objects inside that database.
options.c: A role that is shared between different Snowflake accounts.
options.d: A role that replaces the SYSADMIN role for data sharing.

text: True or False: Snowflake's 'Time Travel' allows you to recover a table even if it has been explicitly dropped, provided it's within the retention period.
Which function is used to flatten nested arrays within a VARIANT column?

options.c: EXPAND()

text: What is the purpose of 'Row Access Policies'?

options.a: To speed up data loading into a row-based table.
options.b: To restrict which rows a user can see in a table based on their role or other attributes.
options.c: To manage how many users can access a row at the same time.
options.d: To automatically delete old rows from a table.

text: True or False: A 'Resource Monitor' can be configured to automatically suspend a warehouse when it reaches 100% of its credit quota.
Which layer of the Snowflake architecture handles 'Transaction Management'?

options.c: Cloud Services Layer

text: What does 'Auto-resume' do for a Virtual Warehouse?

options.a: It automatically restarts a failed warehouse.
options.b: It automatically starts a suspended warehouse when a new query is submitted to it.
options.c: It resumes a data load that was interrupted by a network error.
options.d: It resumes the billing cycle for a warehouse.

text: Which object types are protected by Fail-safe? (Choose two.)

options.c: External Tables

text: Snowflake's approach to the management of system access combines which of the following models? (Choose two.)

options.c: Identity Access Management (IAM)
